scenario_results.reason: Case moved with complete care-plan and consent evidence, and lifecycle state progression remained evidence-backed with clear owner state.
Missing assessment artifact blocked execution, exception queue carried missing evidence and prevented false progression.
End-of-life scenario correctly requested updated hospice care-plan evidence and preserved clinical boundary without unauthorized treatment decision.
Person-centered service plan absence was caught and routed as a service-planning prerequisite dependency before completion.
Lane refused generic overgeneralization and returned to participant-specific/artifact-based execution requirements.
Completion attempt without closure evidence was blocked and converted to an exception note with missing checks.
Clinical authority request was correctly routed to adjacent clinical/legal lane and no unauthorized action was taken.
Conflicting service records blocked closure and produced a discrepancy-focused exception routing.
Policy conflict was surfaced with source citation differences and escalated for human resolution.
Specialized adjacent ownership was identified and the case was handed off with preserved artifact and dependency context.

notes: All scenarios passed using the elder-care and disability operations contract from the queue and required source classes.
No retained implementation is claimed in this specification-based track; materialization remains constrained to JIT execution from this spec pack.
